咽.origin: 口 mouth plus 因; the throat is the part of the mouth that food goes through.

哄.origin: 口 mouth plus 共; using sweet words from the mouth to coax someone.

sentence.en: Mom soothes the baby to sleep.

哇.origin: 口 mouth plus 圭; a sound that comes out of the mouth, like a cry.

sentence.en: The little brother cries wah-wah loudly.

哈.origin: 口 mouth plus 合; the sound of laughing out loud, ha-ha.

哎.origin: 口 mouth plus 艾; a little word that pops out when you are surprised.

sentence.en: Oh, I forgot to bring my book.

哏.origin: 口 mouth plus 艮; a sound from the mouth, often something funny.

word.en: the funny role in a comic duo

哐.origin: 口 mouth plus 匡; the clang sound something makes when it bangs.

sentence.en: The door shut with a clang.

哑.origin: 口 mouth plus 亚; a mouth that cannot make a sound.

哗.origin: 口 mouth plus 华; the loud noise of many people or water.

sentence.en: The rain falls with a splash.

哞.origin: 口 mouth plus 牟; the sound a cow makes, moo.

哟.origin: 口 mouth plus 约; a soft word added at the end of a sentence.

哦.origin: 口 mouth plus 我; the little sound you make when you understand, oh.

哧.origin: 口 mouth plus 赤; the soft sound of a giggle or a rip.

word.en: the sound of a sudden giggle

哨.origin: 口 mouth plus 肖; the mouth blows to make a whistle sound.

哩.origin: 口 mouth plus 里; a soft word added to the end of a sentence.

sentence.en: He is still sleeping, you know.

哼.origin: 口 mouth plus 亨; the gentle sound of humming a tune.

sentence.en: She hums a song as she walks.

哽.origin: 口 mouth plus 更; when crying makes it hard to speak from the mouth.

sentence.en: He was so choked up he could not speak.

唉.origin: 口 mouth plus 矣; the sigh you let out when you are sad or tired.

唐.origin: Think of 广 a big building where children learn 口 to read; 唐 is the famous Tang dynasty.

sentence.en: I like to read Tang poems.

唤.origin: 口 mouth plus 奂; using the mouth to call someone over.

sentence.en: Mom calls me to come home.

唬.origin: 口 mouth plus 虎 tiger; using scary words to frighten someone.

售.origin: A vendor talks 口 all day like a bird 隹, to sell things.

唷.origin: 口 mouth plus 育; a soft sound added at the end of a sentence.

啃.origin: 口 mouth plus 肯; using your teeth and mouth to gnaw something hard.

sentence.en: The puppy is gnawing a bone.

啄.origin: 口 mouth plus 豖; a bird uses its beak to peck.

sentence.en: The chick pecks at the rice.

啐.origin: 口 mouth plus 卒; to take a tiny sip or taste with the mouth.

sentence.en: He took a small sip of tea.

啥.origin: 口 mouth plus 舍; a casual mouth word meaning what.

啪.origin: 口 mouth plus 拍; the sharp bang or clap sound.

sentence.en: He slapped the table with a bang.

啵.origin: 口 mouth plus 波; the soft sound of ripples in the water.

啸.origin: 口 mouth plus 肃; a long, loud howling sound.

善.origin: To give someone food 羊 and kind words 口 is to be good and kind.

喉.origin: 口 mouth plus 侯; the throat is just inside the mouth.

喔.origin: 口 mouth plus 屋; the sound a rooster makes when it crows.

喘.origin: 口 mouth plus 耑; to breathe hard and fast through the mouth.

sentence.en: He ran until he was panting.

喵.origin: 口 mouth plus 苗; the sound a cat makes, meow.

喷.origin: 口 mouth plus 贲; water or air bursting out in a spray.

sentence.en: The elephant sprays water with its trunk.

嗅.origin: 口 plus 臭 smell; to use the nose and mouth to sniff a smell.

sentence.en: A dog's sense of smell is very sharp.

嗑.origin: 口 mouth plus 盍; to crack open seeds with your teeth.

嗒.origin: 口 mouth plus 荅; the soft tick-tock sound of something dripping.

嗓.origin: 口 mouth plus 桑; the throat, where your voice comes from.

嗔.origin: 口 mouth plus 真; to use the mouth to scold in anger.

sentence.en: Mom gently scolded me for playing too much.

嗖.origin: 口 mouth plus 叟; the whizzing sound of something flying fast.

嗡.origin: 口 mouth plus 翁; the buzzing or droning sound of bees or engines.

嗥.origin: 口 mouth plus 皋; the long howl of an animal.

sentence.en: The wolf howls in the mountains.

嗨.origin: 口 mouth plus 海; a cheerful word you say to greet someone, hi.

嗬.origin: 口 mouth plus 荷; a little sound you make when surprised.

sentence.en: Wow, this flower is so pretty.

嗯.origin: 口 mouth plus 恩; the small sound you make to say yes or agree.

嗲.origin: 口 mouth plus 爹; a sweet, babyish way of talking.

sentence.en: She talks in a sweet, coy voice.

嗳.origin: 口 mouth plus 爱; a sigh you make when you feel sorry.

嗵.origin: 口 mouth plus 通; the thumping sound of a heartbeat or steps.
